mark_source_chain: dashboard_server.py _poll_stale_mark(...) calls GLOBAL_STATE_HUB.update_mark(..., source='rest_fallback') when stale REST polling succeeds
dashboard_server.py _stream_positions_mark() snapshots marks and maps mark.get('source') into positions payload field 'mark_source'
dashboard.html applyStreamPositionsMark() copies payload mark_source into latestOpenPaperPositions and card updates
dashboard.html render logic maps rest_fallback -> 'mark REST', poll_fallback -> 'mark POLL', and leaves stream source visually quiet
guard_payload_chain: runtime/state.json is the source read by /api/state for guard-related fields
dashboard.html renderGuardState(s) reads only s.last_guard_result or s.guard and renders raw key/value pairs as guard badges
No guard text is synthesized by dashboard_server.py or dashboard.html beyond generic rendering

current_classification.mark_rest_behavior: Expected fallback behavior. In the current session both polymarket_stream and rest_fallback appear in live SSE, so REST fallback is active as a supplemental recovery path rather than proof of a broken UI binding.
current_classification.fallback_health: Potentially mildly degraded stream freshness on some frames, but not enough evidence here to call it a hard bug. SSE sample showed frame stale=true while at least one position still carried mark_source='polymarket_stream'; dashboard.log also shows active [STALE_POLL] usage, which means the fallback path is engaging in this session.
current_classification.guard_warning_origin: No active guard payload exists in current runtime truth, and no literal 'max down' text was found in the audited runtime/dashboard files. Any displayed max-down style warning likely came from an upstream transient runtime payload or from historical/cached UI state outside the current audited snapshot.

verification_evidence: dashboard_server.py contains log line for [STALE_POLL] and sets source='rest_fallback' in _poll_stale_mark() before positions payload creation.
dashboard.html line regions around render/apply logic map rest_fallback to 'mark REST' and poll_fallback to 'mark POLL'.

Live /api/stream/state sample in this session showed at least one frame with stale=true and a live position carrying mark_source='polymarket_stream'; prior audit sample showed mark_source='rest_fallback' with current_price_stale=false.
runtime/state.json and /api/state currently show null for last_guard_result, guard, last_guard_reason, last_guard_reasons.
Searches of dashboard.html and bot log for 'max down' style text found no literal current runtime/dashboard payload source in the audited files.
residual_risks: Current session evidence confirms the payload/binding chains, but precise fallback frequency quality needs a longer runtime sample if manager wants a stronger 'normal vs degraded' ruling.
If a 'max down' style warning recurs, the exact upstream source likely needs to be captured live at the moment it appears, because current runtime truth contains no guard payload to trace.
